mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 330, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate consumption and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when UNRATE increases by >0.2pp and PAYEMS declines concurrently over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained increase in retail sales and housing starts indicates resilient consumer demand that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize when RSAFS and HOUST both grow >2% sequentially during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 0.5% annually, economic stagnation triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and force negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and persistently high consumer prices creates a stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate earnings and forces negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25pp while CPIAUCSL remains above 325.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a declining unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE declines by >0.3pp over three consecutive months during recovery phases.
